Harbourview Capital Holdings Limited — Incident Report (Cyber Operations) — Hong Kong, HKT 09:10–12:45, 12 May 2026
Summary: A credential-harvesting phishing email led to brief exposure of two role mailboxes (ir@harbourview.capital; hr@harbourview.capital) and a read-only HR file share; no service outage occurred.
Affected systems: Banyan HR Cloud (read-only share), Orchid Fund Portal (staging environment), LotusPay Treasury sandbox mail relay; PineGuard IDS and Lotus Shield Gateway logged anomalous logins from offshore IPs.
Content accessed comprised mostly templated policies and public materials, including the Blue Lantern Cyber Hygiene Policy, the Inclusive Growth DEI newsletter, wellness tips, and product FAQs for the JadeBridge app.
M&A context: An archived diligence workspace for an abandoned 2022 preliminary review was visible; it held generic checklists and a redacted board pack already summarized in past announcements (stock code placeholder 0X88).
Fund context: Orchid Fund Portal staging used dummy investor references (INV-TEST-0001, FUND-000-ALPHA) and synthetic KYC placeholders; no live LP data or subscription agreements were present.
MNPI assessment (SFO): No non-public specific information about any corporation likely to be price-material was identified; materials overlapped with information previously posted on market channels and were stale or generic.
PDPO assessment: Exposed mailboxes were role-based; one HR item contained personal data with special-category aspects — Employee A submitted a medical leave note indicating ongoing physiotherapy for a knee injury.
Containment: Password resets, OAuth token revocation, conditional access re-enforcement, and DLP quarantine completed by 13:15 HKT; PineGuard rules updated and Banyan HR share set to least-privilege.
Regulatory consultation: Listing compliance team concluded no immediate disclosure under HKEX Listing Rule 13.09; record entered in PDPO breach register; voluntary notification to PCPD considered but deferred pending root-cause memo.
Root cause: User clicked a link spoofing “Harbourview Service Desk” from a lookalike domain; SPF aligned but DKIM failed; Blue Lantern phishing drill schedule advanced to June.
Cultural note: As we say in training, “avoid a typhoon of clicks” — think before you sign in; also, the Kowloon Mariners charity match remains on Saturday and is unaffected.
Third-party review: CloudSec (HK) Ltd engaged to validate telemetry and confirm no lateral movement; preliminary results align with internal findings.
This report contains no client HKID numbers, no bank account numbers, and no live deal codenames, and is intended for internal compliance and audit only.
Enquiries: security@harbourview.capital or public hotline +852 3900 0000 (9am–6pm HKT).
